# terrible_openstack_cloud_router (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Name to be give to the router. This router attribute cannot be updated.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `enable_snat` (Boolean) Enable Source NAT (SNAT) attribute.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `external_fixed_ips` (String) The IP address parameters for the external gateway network. Each is a dictionary with the subnet name or ID (subnet) and the IP address to assign on the subnet (ip_address). If no IP is specified, one is automatically assigned from that subnet.
- `external_gateway_info` (String) Information about the router's external gateway
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `interfaces` (String) List of subnets to attach to the router internal interface. Default gateway associated with the subnet will be automatically attached with the router's internal interface. In order to provide an ip address different from the default gateway,parameters are passed as dictionary with keys as network name or ID (*net*), subnet name or ID (*subnet*) and the IP of port (*portip*) from the network. User defined portip is often required when a multiple router need to be connected to a single subnet for which the default gateway has been already used.
- `is_admin_state_up` (Boolean) Desired admin state of the created or existing router.
- `network` (String) Unique name or ID of the external gateway network. Required if *external_fixed_ips* or *enable_snat* are provided. This router attribute cannot be updated.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `project` (String) Unique name or ID of the project. This router attribute cannot be updated.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Indicate desired state of the resource
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `router` (String) Dictionary describing the router.
